Where champions begin. Play for Kangas.
Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club | Play for Kangas
Nunawading Kangas is a junior basketball club with 800+ players across 90+ teams in the Melbourne East Basketball Association Junior Domestic Competition.

With over 50 years of community experience, Nunawading Kangas is a junior basketball club with 800+ players across 90+ teams in the Melbourne East Basketball Association (MEBA) Junior Domestic Competition.
Season Launch Night Brings the Club Together
Players, families, and coaches packed the stadium for a high-energy launch night ahead of the winter season.

The new season kicked off with a packed launch night featuring team introductions, uniform pickups, and a short skills showcase from our junior squads.

The evening gave new families a chance to meet coaches and committee members, while returning players got a preview of upcoming fixtures, training blocks, and community events.

We are especially grateful to our volunteers, who helped the night run smoothly from the registration desk to the canteen.

Holiday Skills Clinic Opens Extra Places
Due to strong demand, the club has added extra places for its upcoming school holiday clinic.

Our holiday clinic focuses on shooting technique, finishing through contact, footwork, and game awareness in small groups.

Players will rotate through skill stations and decision-making games designed to keep sessions energetic and practical.

Registrations remain open until all places are filled.

Community Open Day Set for Saturday Morning
The club will host an open day with half-court games, try-out drills, and a welcome session for new families.

Saturday's open day is designed to introduce the club to local families and help new players find the right program for their age and experience level.

The morning includes mini games, meet-the-coach sessions, and a short parent information briefing covering the season calendar and volunteer roles.

Anyone curious about joining is welcome to attend.

Kangas Coaches Set for Elite Development Session with ShootHQ

Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club is excited to announce a special Coach the Coaches evening in partnership with ShootHQ, the Notting Hill-based training facility backed by some of the finest credentials in Australian basketball.

Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club is excited to announce a special Coach the Coaches evening in partnership with ShootHQ, the Notting Hill-based training facility backed by some of the finest credentials in Australian basketball.

The session, scheduled for Sunday evening in late June, will be led by ShootHQ's Joey Wright - a three-time NBL Coach of the Year - alongside co-founder Adam Gibson, who spent 16 years in the NBL, won championship titles, and represented the Boomers at the 2012 London Olympics and multiple FIBA World Championships. It's a rare opportunity for our coaching community to learn directly from two people who have operated at the very highest levels of the game.

With 94 junior teams across the club this season, Nunawading Kangas has one of the largest and most dedicated volunteer coaching groups in the region. Events like this are a reflection of the club's ongoing commitment to supporting those coaches - because when our coaches grow, our players grow with them.

We're expecting strong interest from across the club and spaces are expected to fill quickly, so stay tuned for registration details coming soon. Whether you're coaching your first season or your fifteenth, this is an evening well worth your time.

A big thank you to Joey, Adam and the team at ShootHQ for making this happen, and to Terence Leong from our Coaching Academy for helping bring it all together.

About the Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club.

Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club is a community based junior club established over 50 years ago. Our players are drawn from Nunawading and the surrounding suburbs. Our aim is to provide an environment for our players to enjoy sport whilst improving their skills, learning about teamwork and respect for other players, coaches and referees. Older players have the opportunity to develop leadership skills through coaching younger teams. We are part of the Melbourne East Basketball Association (MEBA) and have over 800 players (over 90 teams) playing in the Junior Domestic Competitions. ## Our Mission To be premier facilitators of participation, development and excellence in basketball. ## Our Purpose - To select and provide teams to play competitive basketball with other teams and clubs. - To teach basketball skills, tactics and rules to its members. - To provide coaching and adequate training facilities for its members. - To foster and maintain at all times, the highest level of sportsmanship and fair play. - To co-operate with similar clubs to promote and improve the game of basketball. - To further the aim of affiliated basketball association(s) to which we belong. - To provide and promote a range of social activities amongst its membership. - To provide and promote any other sporting activity that shall benefit the community. ## Our Values To promote the following values: - Enjoyment of basketball - Honesty and Integrity - To respect all, regardless of gender, ability, cultural background or religion - Understanding - Openess and Transparency - Teamwork and Co-operation ## Life Members Since our formation in 1974, our Club has been run by a small crew of dedicated volunteers drawn from the families of our players. Many of these volunteers have made significant contributions, far in excess of what was expected of them and over extended years of involvement. Without their involvement, the Club would not have been able to continue to provide the opportunity for kids to play basketball. The Club recognises their much appreciated involvment by bestowing Life Member status on them.

Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club is a community based junior club established over 50 years ago. Our players are drawn from Nunawading and the surrounding suburbs. Our aim is to provide an environment for our players to enjoy sport whilst improving their skills, learning about teamwork and respect for other players, coaches and referees. Older players have the opportunity to develop leadership skills through coaching younger teams.

We are part of the Melbourne East Basketball Association (MEBA) and have over 800 players (over 90 teams) playing in the Junior Domestic Competitions.

Our Mission

To be premier facilitators of participation, development and excellence in basketball.

Our Purpose

  • To select and provide teams to play competitive basketball with other teams and clubs.
  • To teach basketball skills, tactics and rules to its members.
  • To provide coaching and adequate training facilities for its members.
  • To foster and maintain at all times, the highest level of sportsmanship and fair play.
  • To co-operate with similar clubs to promote and improve the game of basketball.
  • To further the aim of affiliated basketball association(s) to which we belong.
  • To provide and promote a range of social activities amongst its membership.
  • To provide and promote any other sporting activity that shall benefit the community.

Our Values

To promote the following values:

  • Enjoyment of basketball
  • Honesty and Integrity
  • To respect all, regardless of gender, ability, cultural background or religion
  • Understanding
  • Openess and Transparency
  • Teamwork and Co-operation

Life Members

Since our formation in 1974, our Club has been run by a small crew of dedicated volunteers drawn from the families of our players. Many of these volunteers have made significant contributions, far in excess of what was expected of them and over extended years of involvement. Without their involvement, the Club would not have been able to continue to provide the opportunity for kids to play basketball.

The Club recognises their much appreciated involvment by bestowing Life Member status on them.

Coaching information for the Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club.

Kangas coaches support the development of our players, help create a positive team environment, and model the standards expected across the club. Coaches must also complete the required screening, training, and conduct requirements before they can coach a team.

## Coach Requirements

The Child Wellbeing and Safety Act 2005 and Victorian child safe requirements place obligations on organisations that work with children and young people. To be successfully selected as a Kangas coach, you must complete the following requirements and provide the correct proof before coaching.

### Working with Children Check

Volunteer coaches must hold a current Working with Children Check. This is free for volunteers and can be started online through the [Victorian Working with Children Check application service](https://service.vic.gov.au/services/working-with-children). After completing the online application, follow the instructions for submitting the application at an Australia Post outlet.

If you already have a Working with Children Check and need to renew it, you can also use the same online service.

Police officers and coaches under 18 years of age are exempt from this requirement, but must provide proof of exemption. Teachers are also exempt, but must [notify Working with Children Check Victoria of the organisations they volunteer with](https://service.vic.gov.au/services/teachers-notifications/).

### Member Protection Declaration

Coaches must complete the Basketball Victoria Member Protection Declaration. The declaration needs to be signed in front of a qualified witness, similar to a statutory declaration. Suitable witnesses include a pharmacist, school principal, or police officer.

Please email [coaching@nunawadingkangas.com.au](mailto:coaching@nunawadingkangas.com.au) if you need the current declaration form.

### Child Protection Course

All coaches must complete the free online PBTR - Child Protection and Safeguarding course provided through Sport Integrity Australia and Play by the Rules. The course takes approximately one hour, can be completed in stages, and provides a certificate on completion.

[Access the child protection course](https://elearning.sportintegrity.gov.au/blocks/androgogic_catalogue/index.php?c1=Courses)

### Code of Conduct

Coaches must abide by the Codes of Conduct adopted by Nunawading Kangas and ensure that players and spectators associated with their team do likewise.

- [MEBA Code of Conduct](https://www.nunawadingbasketball.com.au/About/Code-of-Conduct)
- [Basketball Victoria Codes of Conduct](https://api.basketballvictoria.com.au/api/assets/byuip6j02lssg4ok)
- [Basketball Victoria Coach Hub](https://www.basketballvictoria.com.au/get-involved/coach/hub)

You cannot coach without providing the correct proof. This is Basketball Victoria policy and helps avoid confusion or embarrassment for volunteers, families, and the club.

## Coaching Principles

[Download the Nunawading Kangas Coaches Booklet](/uploads/nunawading-kangas-coaches-booklet.docx)

![Kangas player skill development table](/uploads/coaching-player-skill-development-table.png)

### Value Player Development

Training should balance skill development, game understanding, and team structure. Even with older age groups, where sessions often include more game situations and systems, coaches should keep working to improve players as individuals.

### Create a Positive Environment

For many players, especially in younger age groups, a coach's influence is significant. Long after the results are forgotten, players will remember how they were treated and what kind of environment they were part of.

### Be Part of Your Players' Lives

Be aware of what is happening for players outside basketball. Building trust improves relationships and helps players feel supported. No one will care how much you know until they know how much you care.

### Teach Decision Making

Structure is useful, but young players also need to learn how to make decisions based on what the defence gives them. Instead of only teaching plays, teach players how to play.

Helpful decision-making areas include:

- Reading an on-ball screen
- Reading a dribble handoff
- Reading a basket cut or back cut
- Responding when the defence switches
- Responding when a player cannot catch the ball where the play expects
- Responding to double teams

### Set Clear Defensive Rules

On-ball screens are common in junior basketball, so players need to understand how the team will defend them. Your approach should match the skill, speed, size, and strengths of your team, as well as the opponent.

Common options include:

- Blitzing or trapping the ball carrier
- Icing the screen and forcing the ball toward the baseline
- Switching match-ups
- Showing and recovering

### Win the Extra Point Game

Small areas can add up across a game. Coaches should help teams value:

- Free throws, including drawing fouls and avoiding unnecessary fouls
- Offensive rebounds and limiting opponents to one shot
- Defending open three-point shots and forcing contested attempts
- Fast breaks, including controlled attacking transition and strong defensive transition

### Prepare for Pressure

Every team should have a press breaker. Strong half-court offence and defence can be undone quickly if players do not have structure for beating full-court or half-court pressure.

Baseline and end-line plays are also useful ways to create scoring opportunities without needing to call a timeout.

### Coach Players Fairly and Individually

Players do not all respond to feedback in the same way. Treat players fairly, but recognise that some need reassurance and confidence, while others may need a firmer push to improve effort, focus, or decision making.

## Basketball Victoria Domestic Coaches Clinics

Basketball Victoria runs clinics for domestic coaches throughout the year. These clinics cover skills, drills, and basic coaching philosophy, and often feature experienced coaches such as Kevin Goorjian, Paul Lankford, and Ross Wignell.

[Visit the Basketball Victoria coaching courses and accreditation website](https://www.basketballvictoria.com.au/get-involved/coach/coaching-courses-accreditation)

## External Coaching Resources

We often receive coaching tips and resources that may help our coaching group. If you find something useful, please send it through so it can be shared with others.

- [Developing Post Players](http://team.fastmodelsports.com/2015/09/01/developing-post-players/)
- [Blake Griffin on improving shooting](http://www.theplayerstribune.com/why-aint-he-dunkin/)

For assistance or more information about coaching, email [coaching@nunawadingkangas.com.au](mailto:coaching@nunawadingkangas.com.au).

Be Part of Your Players’ Lives
Fees and uniform information for Nunawading Kangas players and families.

## Winter 2026 Season Fees

Once the official registration period is over, a late fee of $50 will apply to any late registrations allowed.

### Nunawading Kangas Fees

| Fee | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Nunawading Kangas Registration Fee* | $55* |
| Nunawading Kangas Training Fee* | $105* |
| Player contribution to MEBA/GEBC Association Team Sheet Fees | $240 (Monday or Friday) or $215 (Saturday) |

### Other fees payable at registration

| Fee | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| Basketball Victoria Participant Licence fee (junior) *** | $34 + GST*** |
| Basketball Australia Participant fee *** | $5.50 + GST*** |

### Discounts

- Discount for Under 8 players: Our Under 8 players pay only the $55 registration and $240/$215 team sheet fees amounts of the Nunawading Kangas fees. Their $105 training fees are covered by the Club.

- Discount for non-training teams - these players do not pay the $105 training fee.

- Discount for Monday Girls teams - $30 discount from the registration to encourage particatipation.

- *Sibling discount: A 5% discount applies to Nunawading Kangas fees for 2nd and subsequent siblings registered for the same competition, by the same PlayHQ account holder in PlayHQ.*

**Note: Basketball Victoria and Basketball Australia fees are only paid once a year.**

## Explanation of the fee components

1. Registration fees paid to Nunawading Kangas. These fees cover the Club costs including the costs of trophies, Club administration costs and Association registration fees.

2. Training fees paid to Nunawading Kangas. These fees cover the cost of leasing training venues for our teams. Friday teams do not usually train and so those players do not pay this fee. For Friday teams that wish to train, players will be required to pay an additional fee to cover the cost of the training venue for the season. This will be followed up by the Club at the beginning of the season.

3. Team Sheet Fees contribution. Each team pays a `team sheet` fee per game to the Association. Players from the team each pay a contribution towards these fees for the season. The amount of the weekly "team sheet fee" is set by the Association and by venue owners to cover venue, competition and referee costs. This season the Club will be collecting these contributions with registration and paying the fees on behalf of our teams, directly to the Association.

4. Basketball Victoria Participant Licence. Basketball Victoria charges an annual $29 for juniors (17 and under) or $43 for seniors. These fees help cover the costs of managing the sport statewide, which includes the providing of services and insurance to participants, clubs and associations.

5. Basketball Austrialia Paticipant Fee (Game Development Levy) - new in 2024. $5 (plus GST) annual fee, collected by Basketball Australia - the revenue generated will play a pivotal role in sustaining the growth of basketball.

We encourage you to contact the Regsitrar at Registrar@nunawadingkangas.com.au if fee payment up front is an issue your family. It's important to us that kids don't miss out on playing because financial issues.

For more information about joining Nunawading Kangas please contact our Registrar - Registrar@nunawadingkangas.com.au

## Get Active Kids Vouchers

We are able to accept Get Active Kids Vouchers through our association with Basketball Victoria. As each round of vouchers opens, eligibile families are able to either obtain a voucher through Sports and Recreation Victoria priro to completing their PlayHQ registration and use the voucher code provided when registering, or aply to SRV to be reimbursed for fees already paid in the voucher period.

To be eligible players must be 0-18 years old named on a Health Care Card or Pension Concession Card and Medicare Card. Please use Baskeball Victoria (provider number 2951096) as the provider in your voucher application.

For more details on this program please go to [https://www.getactive.vic.gov.au/vouchers/apply-for-vouchers/](https://www.getactive.vic.gov.au/vouchers/apply-for-vouchers/)

## Uniforms

Please note that unless you have a singlet number clash, the club does not provide loan uniforms. For new members, please ensure you order your uniforms as soon as you register as it can take a while for the uniforms to be sent.

If you need a singlet for training or competition, we encourage you to ask around fellow members — someone may have a spare you can borrow.

For grading (first 6 rounds) refs are a lot more lenient which means you can wear any yellow singlet and use black tape to tape a number on. Black shorts, no pockets. However, after grading games, players MUST have the proper playing uniforms otherwise team points will be deducted as pentalty.

We'd like to take a moment to remind everyone that our club is run entirely by volunteers who dedicate their time and effort to ensure things run smoothly for all members. While we understand that issues and frustrations can occasionally arise, please remember that our volunteers deserve to be treated with courtesy and respect at all times.

We're all here because we share a passion for this club and its community. Let's continue to foster a positive, supportive environment where everyone feels valued.

## Uniform Order Process

All players are required to purchase a playing uniform (singlet and shorts) to participate in games. Given the timeframe for manufacturing the uniforms, players need to ensure they order their uniforms ASAP.

Below is the link to order uniforms and merchandise from the club's official apparel supplier:

[Impact Sports Kangas Club Shop](https://www.impactsports.com.au/club/kangas/)

Please note:

To streamline things, we'll now be allocating singlet numbers to players for the life of the club.

- Existing players will retain the number listed in PlayHQ.
- New players will be assigned a number upon joining.

We try to ensure availability across a player's age group, not just the team they are playing in.

The club does not facilitate a second hand uniform operation but should you acquire a second hand uniform, you will need to contact the Uniform Co-ordinator to check that the singlet number is available and if so, register it to the player before it can be worn in games.

Uniform order cycles end on the 15th of each month and take approximately 4-5 weeks to be manufactured.

Cut off date for orders: 15th of each month.

DELIVERY 4 TO 5 WEEKS AFTER CUT OFF DATE

For more details on this program please go to https://www.getactive.vic.gov.au/vouchers/apply-for-vouchers/

Uniforms

Please note that unless you have a singlet number clash, the club does not provide loan uniforms. For new members, please ensure you order your uniforms as soon as you register as it can take a while for the uniforms to be sent.

If you need a singlet for training or competition, we encourage you to ask around fellow members — someone may have a spare you can borrow.

For grading (first 6 rounds) refs are a lot more lenient which means you can wear any yellow singlet and use black tape to tape a number on. Black shorts, no pockets. However, after grading games, players MUST have the proper playing uniforms otherwise team points will be deducted as pentalty.

We’d like to take a moment to remind everyone that our club is run entirely by volunteers who dedicate their time and effort to ensure things run smoothly for all members. While we understand that issues and frustrations can occasionally arise, please remember that our volunteers deserve to be treated with courtesy and respect at all times.

We’re all here because we share a passion for this club and its community. Let’s continue to foster a positive, supportive environment where everyone feels valued.

Uniform Order Process

All players are required to purchase a playing uniform (singlet and shorts) to participate in games. Given the timeframe for manufacturing the uniforms, players need to ensure they order their uniforms ASAP.

Below is the link to order uniforms and merchandise from the club’s official apparel supplier:

Impact Sports Kangas Club Shop

Please note:

To streamline things, we’ll now be allocating singlet numbers to players for the life of the club.

  • Existing players will retain the number listed in PlayHQ.
  • New players will be assigned a number upon joining.

We try to ensure availability across a player’s age group, not just the team they are playing in.

The club does not facilitate a second hand uniform operation but should you acquire a second hand uniform, you will need to contact the Uniform Co-ordinator to check that the singlet number is available and if so, register it to the player before it can be worn in games.

Uniform order cycles end on the 15th of each month and take approximately 4-5 weeks to be manufactured.

Cut off date for orders: 15th of each month.

DELIVERY 4 TO 5 WEEKS AFTER CUT OFF DATE
General enquiry information for the Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club.
Incident reporting information for the Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club.

Nunawading Kangas uses the RevolutioniseSPORT incident report form for online incident submissions.

[Submit an incident report online](https://www.nunawadingkangas.com.au/incident/)

[Download the incident report form](https://www.nunawadingkangas.com.au/incident/download)

## Information You Will Need

The online form asks for:

- Your full name, contact number, and email address
- The incident date and time
- The venue where the incident occurred
- A description of what happened
- The Kangas team involved, if applicable
- The opposition team involved, if applicable
- Any supporting file, such as a photo or document

Supporting files can be up to 10MB. Accepted formats include JPG, GIF, PNG, Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and PDF.

The online form is protected by reCAPTCHA and opens on the current Nunawading Kangas RevolutioniseSPORT site so the report can be submitted securely.

Registration information for Nunawading Kangas players and families.

Winter 2026 Season registration is now closed.

[Click here to go to our registration page on PlayHQ.](#)

[REGISTER TO PLAY WEEKNIGHTS (MONDAY GIRLS OR FRIDAY BOYS)](#)

[REGISTER TO PLAY SATURDAY (BOYS AND GIRLS)](#)

## When to Register?

Winter Season Registration usually opens at the beginning of Term 1, in preparation for the Winter Season that runs over Term 2 and Term 3.

Summer Season registration opens at the beginning of Term 3, in prepartion for the Summer Season that runs over Term 4 and Term 1.

## What if you missed registration?

If you've missed registration, you can still [contact us using this link](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScubmjIHuY0eSvsoOrcW0aBTEaXduTOxbGvtomfI_ZjmUm4cw/viewform) to see if we have any places available for your age group, or to put your name on our contact list to be notified when registration for the next season opens.

## Age groups

Players would usually play in the appropriate age group based on their dob - see the tables below (they may take a second to load) to identify your age group. It's unusual to play out of your age group and you should discuss this with the Age Group Coordinator before you register. Please note that positions in an older grade are subject to availabilty and if numbers do not allow this, the Club may need to place players in their correct age group.

Refund request information for the Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club.
Payment invoice information for the Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club.
Sponsorship information for the Nunawading Kangas Basketball Club.
Training information for Nunawading Kangas players and families.

## Where do we play and train?

| Group | Where | Start Time |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Monday - Junior Under 8 Boys, Mixed or Girls | Nunawading Stadium and other local venues including Forest Hill Secondary College | Games start at 4.15pm, 5pm or 5:45pm |
| Monday - Junior Girls Under 9, 11, 13, 15, 17 & 19 | Nunawading Stadium and other local venues including Forest Hill Secondary College, Mullauna College, Brentwood College, The Knox School | Games start at 5.00pm. 5.45pm |
| Friday - Juniors Under 9 to 19 Boys | Nunawading Stadium, Forest Hill Secondary College, The Knox School and Mullauna Secondary College | Games start at 5.00pm and run through the evening, with younger age groups in the earliest time slots. The last games start around 8.45pm. |
| Friday - Juniors Under 8 to 19 Boys & girls competitions | Nunawading Stadium and various venues in surrounding suburbs including: The Rings, Canterbury Rd, Ringwood; Forest Hill Secondary College, East Burwood; The Knox School, Wantirna South; Mullauna Secondary College, Mitcham; Vermont Secondary College, Vermont; Brentwood Secondary College, Glen Waverley; Huntingtower School, Mount Waverley; Kingswood College, Box Hill South | Games start at 8.00am and run through the day and evening, with younger age groups in the earliest time slots and oldest teams in the later time slots. The last games start at 8.20pm. |

## How Long Is a Game?

Games usually last for around 40 - 45 minutes.

- Saturday games consist of two 20 minute halves, with a 2 minute halftime break.
- Midweek and Friday games consist of two 18 minutes halves with a 2 minute halftime break.

## Is There Training?

### Midweek Teams

Undertake one training session in addition to the game, however our older Midweek girls (Under 19) usually do not train.

### Friday Teams

Do not train, so the time commitment is only to the game each week. There are a small number of Friday teams that do make special arrangements to train, at the discretion of the coach.

### Saturday Teams

Undertake one training session each week in addition to the game.

### Additional Info

Training times are fixed each season on the basis of 1 hour per team between 5.00pm and 10.00pm, usually Wednesdays or Thursdays, with a small number of teams training on Monday or Tuesday.

Training sessions are mostly held at Forest Hill Secondary College, but we also use Nunawading Christian College, Mullauna Secondary College, Orchard Grove Primary School and Vermont Sportslink.

The earlier time slots are allocated to younger teams and training is scheduled to accommodate girls' midweek playing times and coaches' commitments as priorities.

Efforts will be made to fit in with players' commitments, provided that these are advised with each season's registration. However, with over 600 players, some clashes will occur and a player may have to choose between basketball and another activity. Further, the Club has no control over playing times and venues - these are set by the Association with which we are affiliated.
